title: Tissue Glycogen Analysis Protocol
author: John Stack
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.y39fyr6

steps.title: Obtain samples of around 25mg
steps.instruction: Obtain samples of around 25mg. Keep samples in liquid nitrogen until ready to weigh.

steps.title: Multiply weight of tissue by 5,...
steps.instruction: Multiply weight of tissue by 5, and add that amount of 0.6N perchloric acid (PCA) to labeled 12x75mm plastic shaker tubes. Keep these tubes on ice.

steps.title: Once done with above, add tissue...
steps.instruction: Once done with above, add tissue to plastic homogenizing tubes and homogenize in shaker at 20Hz for 30 seconds. Do not throw out the PCA homogenized mixture until results are calculated.

steps.title: Add 50µL of PCA homogenate to labeled microfuge tubes
steps.instruction: Add 50µL of PCA homogenate to labeled microfuge tubes.

steps.title: Prepare fresh Amyloglucosidase solution (Amylogucosidase and...
steps.instruction: Prepare fresh Amyloglucosidase solution (Amylogucosidase and Acetate Buffer in a 2mg enzyme /1mL buffer ratio. Pipette 125 µL of fresh amyloglucosidase into each microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Glycogen content analysis 12) Measure the...
steps.instruction: Glycogen content analysis 12) Measure the amount of glucose (Cobas Mira Glucose method) in the 2hr-amyloglucosidase digested samples.

steps.title: Glycogen Concentration Calculation 13) Glycogen expressed...
steps.instruction: Glycogen Concentration Calculation 13) Glycogen expressed as the gm % glycogen in tissue (i.e.,weight percentage of liver) is calculated as :

description: Background (free glucose in tissue homogenate) 10) While glycogen samples are incubating, centrifuge tubes containing PCA homogenate for 1 minute at 10,000 rpm. 11) Measure the glucose concentration of the PCA homogenate solution (Cobas Mira Glucose method) to obtain the amount of free glucose in each sample.
